exercises.title: Inspect your interfaces
exercises.description: Run ip addr and identify your active interface, IP address, and netmask.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can point to the interface name, IPv4 address, and prefix length.

exercises.title: Trace a route
exercises.description: Run traceroute or tracepath to a public host and identify at least three hops.
exercises.expected_outcome: You see TTL-based hop discovery and per-hop round-trip times.

exercises.title: Build a subnet calculator
exercises.description: Write a small script that converts a CIDR into network, broadcast, and usable host range.
exercises.expected_outcome: The script prints correct network math for arbitrary prefixes.

exercises.title: Capture ARP traffic
exercises.description: Use tcpdump while pinging a host on your LAN and identify ARP resolution before the ICMP echo.
exercises.command: sudo tcpdump -i any -n arp or icmp
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see ARP request/reply frames and the following ICMP exchange.

exercises.title: Implement longest-prefix routing
exercises.description: Write a small router simulator that stores CIDR routes and picks the best match for a destination IP.
exercises.expected_outcome: Lookups choose the most specific prefix and fall back to a default route.

exercises.title: Build a packet path explainer
exercises.description: Create a script that takes a source, destination, and protocol and prints the OSI layers involved end to end.
exercises.expected_outcome: The output shows encapsulation and forwarding decisions per layer.
